---
title: "Check models performance"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Environment and parameter loading

This section loads the statistical engine and the parameters determined during the sensitivity analysis phase to ensure consistency.

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| message: false

invisible(Sys.setlocale("LC_TIME", "C"))
libs <- c("tidyverse", "lubridate", "piecewiseSEM", "performance", "lme4", 
          "purrr", "nlme", "moments", "car", "jsonlite", "knitr")
invisible(lapply(libs, require, character.only = TRUE))

# Load Global Parameters (from the sensitivity analysis)
if(file.exists("../output/model_parameters.txt")) {
  best_params <- read_json("../output/model_parameters.txt")
  WIN_DAYS   <- as.numeric(best_params$selected_window)
  MIN_RATE   <- as.numeric(best_params$selected_min_rate)
  TRANSFO_ROOT  <- as.character(best_params$transform_root)
  TRANSFO_FAUNA <- as.character(best_params$transform_fauna)
} else {
  # Default safety parameters if config file is missing
  WIN_DAYS <- 14; MIN_RATE <- 0.3; TRANSFO_ROOT <- "sqrt_plus_one"; TRANSFO_FAUNA <- "sqrt_plus_one"
  warning("Configuration file not found. Using default fallback parameters.")
}

message(paste0("Using Optimized Config: Window=", WIN_DAYS, "d, Rate=", MIN_RATE, ", Transfo root=", TRANSFO_ROOT, ", Transfo fauna=", TRANSFO_FAUNA))
```

## Data loading and preprocessing

Handles data cleaning, variable mapping, and ecological transformations.

```{r}
#| label: data-preparation

# Load Raw Database
data_sem_raw <- read.csv("../output/SEM_database.csv", sep = ",", na.strings = c("", "NA")) %>%
  mutate(
    position = as.factor(position),
    depth    = as.factor(depth),
    date     = ymd_hms(date, tz = "UTC"),
    root = root_cm2_count)

# Load Metadata
fauna_vars         <- c("all_taxa", readLines("../output/fauna_vars.txt"))
microclimate_index <- readLines("../output/microclimate_index.txt")

# Apply Chosen Transformation
# Helper function for individual transformations
apply_transfo <- function(x, method) {
  if (is.na(method) || method == "none") return(x)
  if (method == "sqrt_plus_one") return(sqrt(x) + sqrt(x + 1))
  if (method == "log")           return(log1p(x))
  if (method == "hellinger")     return(sqrt(x / sum(x, na.rm = TRUE)))
  if (method == "box_cox")       return(((x + 1)^0.5 - 1) / 0.5)
  return(x)
}
#Apply Differential Transformations
data_transformed <- data_sem_raw %>%
  # Transformation de la racine
  mutate(root = apply_transfo(root, TRANSFO_ROOT)) %>%
  # Transformation de la faune (toutes les colonnes de faune)
  mutate(across(all_of(intersect(fauna_vars, names(.))), 
                ~ apply_transfo(., TRANSFO_FAUNA))) %>%
  # Standardize all numeric data to Z-scores (Mean=0, SD=1)
  # This allows direct comparison of 'path coefficients' between different units.
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ as.numeric(scale(.)))) 
```

## Analytical functions

```{r}
#| label: rolling-window-generator
# Creates list of data slices based on a sliding time window
create_rolling_windows <- function(df, window_days, step_days = 1) {
  start_dates <- seq(min(df$date), 
                     max(df$date) - days(window_days) + hours(6), 
                     by = paste(step_days, "days"))
  
  windows <- map(start_dates, ~ filter(df, date >= .x & date < .x + days(window_days)))
  names(windows) <- format(start_dates, "%Y-%m-%d")
  return(windows)
}
```

```{r}
#| label: SEM-structure
# This function defines the SEM structure applied to each
# rolling window. The structure is fixed across all taxa,
# positions, and time windows.
#
# Components:
# - root biomass explained by microclimate
# - fauna explained by microclimate and root
# - random effects nested by orientation / depth
# - AR(1) temporal autocorrelation 
fit_psem <- function(data, m_indices) {
  psem(
    lme(root ~ microclimate_1 + microclimate_2, data = data,
        random = ~ 1 | orientation / depth, 
        correlation = corAR1(form = ~ 1 | orientation / depth)),
    lme(fauna ~ microclimate_1 + microclimate_2 + root, data = data,
        random = ~ 1 | orientation / depth, 
        correlation = corAR1(form = ~ 1 | orientation / depth)),
    microclimate_1 %~~% microclimate_2
  )
}
```

```{r}
#| label: fixed-pipeline
# Pipeline Wrapper : Orchestrates data filtering and model fitting per taxon/position

run_sem_pipeline <- function(taxon_var, data_raw, m_indices, 
                             window_days, min_rate) {
  
  cat("\n--- Processing Taxon:", taxon_var, "---\n")
  # Standardize column names for the SEM formula
  sem_data <- data_raw %>%
    mutate(fauna = !!sym(taxon_var),
           microclimate_1 = !!sym(m_indices[1]),
           microclimate_2 = !!sym(m_indices[2])) %>%
    select(date, position, orientation, depth, fauna, root, microclimate_1, microclimate_2)
  
  positions <- unique(sem_data$position)

  map_dfr(positions, function(pos) {
    df_pos  <- filter(sem_data, position == pos)
    windows <- create_rolling_windows(df_pos, window_days, step_days = 1)
    
    if(length(windows) == 0) return(NULL)
    
    # QUALITY FILTER: Only keep windows with enough successful sensor readings
    # Window_days * 16 readings per day is the theoretical max as we have 4 images per day according to 2 depths and 2 orientations
    valid_wins <- keep(windows, ~ (nrow(drop_na(.x)) / (window_days * 16)) >= min_rate)
    
    cat(paste0("Pos ", pos, ": ", length(valid_wins), "/", length(windows), " windows passed filter.\n"))
    
    if(length(valid_wins) == 0) return(NULL)
    
    map_dfr(names(valid_wins), function(w_name) {
      win_data <- drop_na(valid_wins[[w_name]])
      
      # Variance Check: Models fail if data is constant (e.g., all zeros)
      if(sd(win_data$fauna) == 0 || sd(win_data$root) == 0) return(NULL)
      
      # Fit SEM and catch convergence errors
      model <- tryCatch(fit_psem(win_data, m_indices), error = function(e) NULL)
      if (is.null(model)) return(NULL)
      
      # Summary Extraction
      sm <- summary(model)
      coefs <- as.data.frame(sm$coefficients)

      # Extraction of R-Squared (Marginal = fixed effects only, Conditional = fixed + random)      
      r2_raw <- as.data.frame(sm$R2)
      r2_df <- r2_raw %>%
        mutate(r2_cond = .[[grep("R.squared|Conditional", names(.))[1]]],
               r2_marg = .[[grep("Marginal", names(.))[1]]]) %>%
        select(response = Response, r2_cond, r2_marg)
      
      tibble(
        taxon = taxon_var, position = pos, date_start = as.Date(w_name),
        n_obs = sm$n, response = coefs$Response, predictor = coefs$Predictor,
        estimate = coefs$Estimate, std_estimate = coefs$Std.Estimate,
        p_value = as.numeric(str_replace(coefs$P.Value, "<", ""))
      ) %>% left_join(r2_df, by = "response")
    })
  })
}
```

## Model execution

```{r}
#| label: execution

# A. Generate Observed Results
message(">>> Running Observed Models...")
sem_results_raw <- map_dfr(fauna_vars, ~ run_sem_pipeline(.x, data_transformed, microclimate_index, WIN_DAYS, MIN_RATE))

# B. Generate Null Distribution (Permutation)
# Permute the data to create a baseline of "random chance"
# This helps confirm that your observed path coefficients are not just noise.
n_perm <- 30
message(">>> Generating Null Distribution (n=30)...")
sem_results_null <- map_dfr(1:n_perm, function(i) {
  # Shuffling variables independently breaks the causal links
  data_null <- data_transformed %>% 
    mutate(across(all_of(c(fauna_vars, "root", microclimate_index)), ~sample(.)))
  
  # Run on 'all_taxa' as a representative for null baseline
  run_sem_pipeline(fauna_vars[1], data_null, microclimate_index, WIN_DAYS, MIN_RATE) %>%
    mutate(iteration = i)
})

# Summarize Null Baseline for comparison
sem_null_summary <- sem_results_null %>%
  group_by(response, predictor) %>%
  summarise(
    null_mean_estimate = mean(estimate, na.rm = TRUE),
    null_sd_estimate   = sd(estimate, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```

##Consolidation and export

```{r}
#| label: consolidation

# Merge Observed and Null Summaries
sem_results <- sem_results_raw %>%
  mutate(type = "observed") %>%
  left_join(sem_null_summary, by = c("response", "predictor")) %>%
  # Filter out correlated errors (~~) to keep only causal paths (->)
  filter(!str_detect(predictor, "~~")) %>%
  mutate(path_id = paste(predictor, "->", response))

# Season Mapping Helper
add_seasons <- function(df) {
  seasons <- tibble(
    month = 1:12,
    season = c("Winter","Winter","Spring","Spring","Spring","Summer","Summer","Summer","Autumn","Autumn","Autumn","Winter")
  )
  df %>% mutate(month = month(date_start)) %>% left_join(seasons, by = "month")
}

sem_results <- add_seasons(sem_results)

# Final Save
write.csv(sem_results, "../output/SEM_results_database.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```
